name: d4-site-template
description: Base Next.js site shell: layout, mega-menu header (nav groups with children render full-width panels; flat navs render as plain links), dark/light mode with a contrast-validated dark palette per pairing/preset, optional announcement bar, site-wide quote-request modal wired to the contact route, nav-driven footer with social links, shared UI (FaqAccordion, LogoMarquee, ProductCard), home/about/contact pages, theme via CSS variables. Every assembled site starts from this template; feature modules copy in on top of it.
clientFacingSummary: A fast, modern marketing site with a full mega-menu navigation, light and dark mode, an announcement bar, a quote-request form, and a contact form that delivers inquiries to your inbox.

env.name: RESEND_API_KEY
env.description: Resend API key for contact form email delivery. If unset, submissions are stored in data/messages.json instead.

env.name: CONTACT_TO_EMAIL
env.description: Destination inbox for contact form submissions when RESEND_API_KEY is set.

env.name: TURSO_DATABASE_URL
env.description: libsql:// URL for this client's Turso database, used as the contact-form fallback store when RESEND_API_KEY is unset. Falls back to a local SQLite file under data/cms.db in dev.

env.name: TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN
env.description: Auth token for TURSO_DATABASE_URL. Required whenever TURSO_DATABASE_URL is set.

env.name: NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL
env.description: Canonical public origin (https://clientdomain.com) used by sitemap.xml and robots.txt. Falls back to the Vercel deployment URL, then localhost.

postAssemble.notes: The builder rewrites src/config/nav.generated.ts with nav entries from every selected module, rewrites src/config/site.ts (identity + nav structure + announcement + quote config + social links) from the build config, rewrites src/app/theme.css (light :root block plus a .dark block when the palette source has a contrast-validated dark variant), and rewrites src/config/fonts.generated.ts + src/config/design.generated.ts (font pairing, motion signature, darkMode flag). Nav items may carry description + children; groups with children render as mega-menu panels. The checked-in defaults are the modern-signal pairing (light + dark) so the template runs standalone.
